content.verdict: Heritage Hotel Stari Grad occupies a lovingly restored 15th-century Venetian palazzo in Stari Grad — the oldest continuously inhabited town in Croatia and one of the most unspoiled on the entire Dalmatian coast. Unlike glamorous Hvar Town 20km away, Stari Grad has retained a genuine village soul: cats sleep on stone steps, fishermen mend nets in the harbour, and lavender fields carpet the surrounding plain. This hotel is perfect for honeymooners who want historic grandeur, authentic Croatian culture, and tranquillity, without the party atmosphere and crowds that define Hvar Town in summer.
content.best_room: Book a Suite in the historic palazzo wing — these rooms have original stone vaulted ceilings, antique Dalmatian furnishings alongside contemporary comforts, and views over either the old town rooftops or the sea. The upper-floor suites are the most romantic, combining genuine historical character with modern bathrooms and air conditioning. Ask specifically for a room with a stone-arched window overlooking the harbour.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Stari Grad Arrival
itinerary_7_nights.description: Arrive by catamaran from Split directly into Stari Grad harbour — one of the great arrivals in Croatia, the ferry slipping into the long, protected bay past vineyards and stone villages. Your hotel is steps from the harbour. Order local Bogdanuša white wine on the hotel's terrace and let the quiet beauty of the town settle over you before a dinner of fresh fish at a harbourside konoba.

itinerary_7_nights.title: The UNESCO Plain & Wine Tasting
itinerary_7_nights.description: Rent bicycles and cycle out through the Stari Grad Plain — a UNESCO World Heritage landscape of ancient Greek field divisions, dry-stone walls, and vine and olive cultivation unchanged for 2,400 years. Visit a local winery such as Plančić or Domaine Duboković for a tasting of Bogdanuša and Plavac Mali, then return through fields of lavender for a spa treatment at the hotel.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Hvar Town Day
itinerary_7_nights.description: Take the local bus or taxi to Hvar Town for the day — enjoy the glamour, the Cathedral, the fortress, and the stylish harbour scene without having to stay there. The contrast with Stari Grad's quiet authenticity makes each town better appreciated. Return in the afternoon before the day-tripper crowds depart and find Stari Grad suddenly entirely your own.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Sailing the Inner Islands
itinerary_7_nights.description: The hotel concierge can arrange a private sailing charter from Stari Grad Bay into the channels between Hvar and the islands of Šćedro and Korčula. The inner sea between the islands is sheltered, warm, and extraordinarily beautiful — anchor in a cove on Šćedro island for a private swim, eat lunch on deck, and return by late afternoon for a couples' treatment at the spa.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Spa & Lavender Day
itinerary_7_nights.description: A day dedicated to rest and the hotel's wellness offering — morning hydrotherapy and a Croatian lavender and rosemary massage, followed by lunch on the terrace. In the afternoon, walk the lavender routes around the town as the afternoon heat brings out the scent of the fields. The hotel can arrange an evening candlelit dinner in the palazzo's private courtyard.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Island Interior Trek
itinerary_7_nights.description: Hike or drive into the island's interior — Hvar's limestone karst highlands are dramatic and little-visited, with abandoned stone villages, wild herbs underfoot, and extraordinary views to both coasts. Pack a picnic from the hotel's kitchen and make a day of it. Return via the south coast road through the wine villages of Svirče and Vrboska.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Farewell by the Harbour
itinerary_7_nights.description: Last morning in Stari Grad: a slow breakfast in the hotel garden, a final walk along the ancient harbour walls, and perhaps one more glass of local white wine as the morning ferry loads. Stari Grad's quiet human scale, its Roman and Greek stones, and the Adriatic light make leaving genuinely hard. The catamaran back to Split carries you past the whole beautiful coast.

caveats: Stari Grad is quieter and less glamorous than Hvar Town — couples who want nightlife, a buzzy scene, or proximity to the famous Carpe Diem beach bar should be aware they are choosing the authentic, quieter alternative.
The hotel is a historic building and some rooms in the older wing have quirks (steep stone stairs, irregular layouts) — discuss any accessibility needs with the hotel before booking.
Beach access requires a short drive or taxi ride as the town harbour is not a swimming beach; the hotel can arrange transport to the nearest beaches.

faqs.question: Is Heritage Hotel Stari Grad good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: It is one of the most authentic and romantic choices on the Dalmatian coast, especially for couples who are seeking culture, history, and genuine Croatian atmosphere over beach-resort luxury. The 15th-century palazzo, lavender fields, UNESCO Plain, and excellent local cuisine create a honeymoon that will be remembered for its depth rather than just its beauty.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit?
faqs.answer: June and September are ideal — the lavender peaks in June (an extraordinary sight and smell), the sea is warm, and Stari Grad is pleasantly unhurried. July and August are the warmest months and the hotel is busy, but even in peak summer Stari Grad remains far quieter than Hvar Town. The hotel opens April through October.

faqs.question: What should we book in advance?
faqs.answer: Book a palazzo suite well in advance for summer — these rooms are in high demand and the hotel is small. Arrange any sailing charters and private dining experiences with the concierge before arrival. If visiting in June, confirm lavender bloom dates as they vary slightly year to year.

faqs.question: Is it adults-only?
faqs.answer: No, the hotel accepts all guests. In practice the sophisticated historic setting and the lack of children's facilities means the atmosphere is predominantly adult and couples-focused. During the summer the clientele is mostly couples, cultural tourists, and sailing visitors.

faqs.question: What's the best room type for a honeymoon?
faqs.answer: A Suite in the historic palazzo wing with original stone features and a sea or town view is the definitive honeymoon choice. These rooms combine genuine historical character — stone arches, antique details — with full modern comforts. Upper floors offer better views and more privacy. Discuss specific room options with the hotel directly as each is unique.

faqs.question: How do we get there?
faqs.answer: Fly into Split Airport (SPU) and take the fast catamaran directly to Stari Grad harbour — the service runs several times daily in summer and takes around 1 hour. This is the most scenic and convenient arrival. Alternatively, a car ferry runs from Split and Drvenik to Stari Grad. The hotel is within easy walking distance of the harbour.
